message.content:
Audit the CourtOps Tennis tournament-director app at `C:\Users\vkozy\repos\tennis-app` for design, UX, accessibility, and code-quality issues. This is a USTA tournament back-office POC: FastAPI + psycopg backend, Postgres, pure HTML/CSS + vanilla JS frontend (no build), Tabulator 6.3.1 vendored. Admin role uses cookie sessions.

Key files to look at:
- `frontend/index.html` — all panels, forms, modals (~1500 lines)
- `frontend/app.js` — wireEntity, makeListGrid, panel rendering (~2700 lines)
- `frontend/styles.css` — design tokens, layout, modals, grid
- `backend/app/main.py`, `backend/app/routers/*.py`, `backend/app/models.py`
- `docs/audit.md`, `docs/data-model.md`, `docs/roadmap.md` for intent

The app has Setup tabs (tournaments/sites/officials/players/rates/hotels/distances/divisions/events) and per-tournament workspace tabs (sites, roster, late entries, withdrawals, scheduling, division-flex, player-hotels, doubles, pairing-avoidances, room-blocks, availability, assignments, reports, t-shirts, inbox, import). Recent work added gender-aware division/event pickers, required birthdate, CSV export with importable columns, 32-player demo seed (16 boys/16 girls).

What I want from you:
1. **Critical issues** (data integrity, broken UX, accessibility blockers, security smells beyond CSRF which is deferred).
2. **Moderate issues** (inconsistent patterns, friction points, places the code is doing too much, duplicated logic, weak error handling).
3. **Polish opportunities** (visual consistency, microcopy, keyboard nav, empty states, loading states).
4. **Architectural drift** (places the wireEntity / makeListGrid abstractions strain, where the frontend has grown organically and could be consolidated).

Look at actual code — don't repeat what audit.md already says is done. Skim the file sizes first (line counts) so you know which files are biggest, then focus there. Be specific: give file paths + line numbers + concrete fix suggestions, not vague "consider improving X".

Report as a prioritized list with sections for Critical / Moderate / Polish / Architecture. Aim for a useful ~30-60 item list — substantive issues only, no padding. Under 1500 words total.

attachment.content:
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- design:user-research: Plan, conduct, and synthesize user research. Trigger with "user research plan", "interview guide", "usability test", "survey design", "research questions", or when the user needs help with any aspect of understanding their users through research.
- design:accessibility-review: Run a WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit on a design or page. Trigger with "audit accessibility", "check a11y", "is this accessible?", or when reviewing a design for color contrast, keyboard navigation, touch target size, or screen reader behavior before handoff.
- design:design-handoff: Generate developer handoff specs from a design. Use when a design is ready for engineering and needs a spec sheet covering layout, design tokens, component props, interaction states, responsive breakpoints, edge cases, and animation details.
- design:ux-copy: Write or review UX copy — microcopy, error messages, empty states, CTAs. Trigger with "write copy for", "what should this button say?", "review this error message", or when naming a CTA, wording a confirmation dialog, filling an empty state, or writing onboarding text.
- design:design-system: Audit, document, or extend your design system. Use when checking for naming inconsistencies or hardcoded values across components, writing documentation for a component's variants, states, and accessibility notes, or designing a new pattern that fits the existing system.
- design:design-critique: Get structured design feedback on usability, hierarchy, and consistency. Trigger with "review this design", "critique this mockup", "what do you think of this screen?", or when sharing a Figma link or screenshot for feedback at any stage from exploration to final polish.
- design:research-synthesis: Synthesize user research into themes, insights, and recommendations. Use when you have interview transcripts, survey results, usability test notes, support tickets, or NPS responses that need to be distilled into patterns, user segments, and prioritized next steps.
- data:data-visualization: Create effective data visualizations with Python (matplotlib, seaborn, plotly). Use when building charts, choosing the right chart type for a dataset, creating publication-quality figures, or applying design principles like accessibility and color theory.
- data:write-query: Write optimized SQL for your dialect with best practices. Use when translating a natural-language data need into SQL, building a multi-CTE query with joins and aggregations, optimizing a query against a large partitioned table, or getting dialect-specific syntax for Snowflake, BigQuery, Postgres, etc.
- data:sql-queries: Write correct, performant SQL across all major data warehouse dialects (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, PostgreSQL, etc.). Use when writing queries, optimizing slow SQL, translating between dialects, or building complex analytical queries with CTEs, window functions, or aggregations.
- data:create-viz: Create publication-quality visualizations with Python. Use when turning query results or a DataFrame into a chart, selecting the right chart type for a trend or comparison, generating a plot for a report or presentation, or needing an interactive chart with hover and zoom.
- data:data-context-extractor: Generate or improve a company-specific data analysis skill by extracting tribal knowledge from analysts.
BOOTSTRAP MODE - Triggers: "Create a data context skill", "Set up data analysis for our warehouse", "Help me create a skill for our database", "Generate a data skill for [company]" → Discovers schemas, asks key questions, generates initial skill with reference files
ITERATION MODE - Triggers: "Add context about [domain]", "The skill needs more info about [topic]", "Update the data skill with [metrics/tables/terminology]", "Improve the [domain] reference" → Loads existing skill, asks targeted questions, appends/updates reference files
Use when data analysts want Claude to understand their company's specific data warehouse, terminology, metrics definitions, and common query patterns.
- data:explore-data: Profile and explore a dataset to understand its shape, quality, and patterns. Use when encountering a new table or file, checking null rates and column distributions, spotting data quality issues like duplicates or suspicious values, or deciding which dimensions and metrics to analyze.
- data:build-dashboard: Build an interactive HTML dashboard with charts, filters, and tables. Use when creating an executive overview with KPI cards, turning query results into a shareable self-contained report, building a team monitoring snapshot, or needing multiple charts with filters in one browser-openable file.
- data:statistical-analysis: Apply statistical methods including descriptive stats, trend analysis, outlier detection, and hypothesis testing. Use when analyzing distributions, testing for significance, detecting anomalies, computing correlations, or interpreting statistical results.
- data:analyze: Answer data questions -- from quick lookups to full analyses. Use when looking up a single metric, investigating what's driving a trend or drop, comparing segments over time, or preparing a formal data report for stakeholders.
- data:validate-data: QA an analysis before sharing -- methodology, accuracy, and bias checks. Use when reviewing an analysis before a stakeholder presentation, spot-checking calculations and aggregation logic, verifying a SQL query's results look right, or assessing whether conclusions are actually supported by the data.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll audit the CourtOps Tennis app systematically. Let me start by understanding the codebase structure and file sizes.

input.command: cd /c/Users/vkozy/repos/tennis-app && find . -type f \( -name "*.py" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.html" -o -name "*.css" \) -not -path "./.venv/*" -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/vendor/*" -not -path "*/__pycache__/*" | xargs wc -l 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -40

content.text: Now let me look at the backend and CSS briefly.

content.content:
1	"""POC authentication helpers: pbkdf2 password hashing + cookie-session lookup.
2	
3	Not production-grade (localhost POC) but real: salted pbkdf2-sha256 hashes, a
4	server-side session token in an HttpOnly cookie, role-based dependencies.
5	"""
6	import hashlib
7	import hmac
8	import secrets
9	
10	from fastapi import Cookie, Depends, HTTPException
11	
12	from .db import db_dep
13	
14	COOKIE_NAME = "sid"
15	_ITERATIONS = 200_000
16	
17	
18	def hash_pw(password: str) -> str:
19	    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
20	    dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), bytes.fromhex(salt), _ITERATIONS)
21	    return f"pbkdf2_sha256${_ITERATIONS}${salt}${dk.hex()}"
22	
23	
24	def verify_pw(password: str, stored: str) -> bool:
25	    try:
26	        _algo, iters, salt, h = stored.split("$")
27	        dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), bytes.fromhex(salt), int(iters))
28	        return hmac.compare_digest(dk.hex(), h)
29	    except Exception:
30	        return False
31	
32	
33	def get_current_user(sid: str | None = Cookie(default=None), conn=Depends(db_dep)) -> dict:
34	    if not sid:
35	        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="not authenticated")
36	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
37	        cur.execute(
38	            "SELECT u.id, u.username, u.role, u.official_id "
39	            "FROM session s JOIN user_account u ON u.id = s.user_id "
40	            "WHERE s.token = %s AND s.expires_at > now()",
41	            (sid,),
42	        )
43	        user = cur.fetchone()
44	    if user is None:
45	        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="not authenticated")
46	    return user
47	
48	
49	def require_admin(user=Depends(get_current_user)) -> dict:
50	    if user["role"] != "admin":
51	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="admin only")
52	    return user
53

1	"""CourtOps Tennis — Phase 0 API + static frontend.
2	
3	Run from the backend/ dir:  uvicorn app.main:app --reload
4	Serves the JSON API under /api and the pure HTML/CSS frontend at /.
5	"""
6	from pathlib import Path
7	
8	from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI
9	from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
10	
11	from .routers import (
12	    adult_lists,
13	    assignments,
14	    auth,
15	    availability,
16	    certifications,
17	    distances,
18	    divisions,
19	    doubles,
20	    emails,
21	    health,
22	    hotels,
23	    imports,
24	    late_entries,
25	    me,
26	    officials,
27	    pairing_avoidances,
28	    player_hotels,
29	    players,
30	    rates,
31	    reports,
32	    room_blocks,
33	    roster,
34	    sites,
35	    tournaments,
36	    withdrawals,
37	)
38	from .security import require_admin
39	
40	app = FastAPI(title="CourtOps Tennis API", version="0.4.0")
41	
42	# Open endpoints: health + auth (login) + the official self-service surface
43	# (which checks the session itself).
44	app.include_router(health.router)
45	app.include_router(auth.router)
46	app.include_router(me.router)
47	
48	# Admin-only: every TD/back-office router requires an admin session.
49	_admin = [Depends(require_admin)]
50	for r in (sites, tournaments, officials, players, rates, hotels, room_blocks,
51	          distances, divisions, roster, assignments, reports, certifications, availability,
52	          emails, late_entries, withdrawals, adult_lists, player_hotels,
53	          pairing_avoidances, doubles, imports):
54	    app.include_router(r.router, dependencies=_admin)
55	
56	# Serve the pure HTML/CSS frontend (repo-root/frontend) at "/".
57	_FRONTEND = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "frontend"
58	if _FRONTEND.is_dir():
59	    app.mount("/", StaticFiles(directory=str(_FRONTEND), html=True), name="frontend")
60

1	"""Login / logout / me — cookie-session auth (POC)."""
2	import secrets
3	
4	from fastapi import APIRouter, Cookie, Depends, HTTPException, Response
5	
6	from ..db import db_dep
7	from ..models import LoginIn
8	from ..security import COOKIE_NAME, get_current_user, verify_pw
9	
10	router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/auth", tags=["auth"])
11	
12	
13	@router.post("/login")
14	def login(body: LoginIn, response: Response, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
15	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
16	        cur.execute(
17	            "SELECT id, username, password_hash, role, official_id "
18	            "FROM user_account WHERE username = %s",
19	            (body.username,),
20	        )
21	        user = cur.fetchone()
22	        if user is None or not verify_pw(body.password, user["password_hash"]):
23	            raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="invalid username or password")
24	        cur.execute("DELETE FROM session WHERE expires_at <= now()")  # opportunistic cleanup
25	        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
26	        cur.execute(
27	            "INSERT INTO session (token, user_id, expires_at) "
28	            "VALUES (%s, %s, now() + interval '30 days')",
29	            (token, user["id"]),
30	        )
31	    response.set_cookie(COOKIE_NAME, token, httponly=True, samesite="lax", path="/")
32	    return {"username": user["username"], "role": user["role"], "official_id": user["official_id"]}
33	
34	
35	@router.post("/logout")
36	def logout(response: Response, sid: str | None = Cookie(default=None), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
37	    if sid:
38	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
39	            cur.execute("DELETE FROM session WHERE token = %s", (sid,))
40	    response.delete_cookie(COOKIE_NAME, path="/")
41	    return {"ok": True}
42	
43	
44	@router.get("/me")
45	def me(user=Depends(get_current_user)):
46	    return {"username": user["username"], "role": user["role"], "official_id": user["official_id"]}
47

1	"""Tournament <-> Player roster (tournament_entry) + CSV/XLSX import."""
2	import csv
3	import io
4	import re
5	
6	import psycopg
7	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, File, HTTPException, Response, UploadFile
8	from openpyxl import load_workbook
9	
10	from ..db import db_dep
11	from ..models import RosterEntryCreate, RosterEntryOut
12	from ..playerops import upsert_player
13	
14	router = APIRouter(tags=["roster"])
15	
16	# Map many header spellings (USTA exports vary) to canonical roster fields (§3.8).
17	_HEADER_ALIASES = {
18	    "usta_number": {"ustanumber", "usta", "ustano", "ustaid", "usta"},
19	    "first_name": {"firstname", "first", "givenname"},
20	    "last_name": {"lastname", "last", "surname", "familyname"},
21	    "age_division": {"agedivision", "division", "div", "age"},
22	    "events": {"events", "event"},
23	    "selection_status": {"selectionstatus", "status", "selection"},
24	    "t_shirt_size": {"tshirtsize", "tshirt", "shirt", "shirtsize", "size"},
25	    "dietary_preference": {"dietarypreference", "dietary", "diet", "dietaryrestrictions"},
26	}
27	_VALID_STATUS = {"selected", "alternate", "withdrawn"}
28	
29	
30	def _norm(h: str) -> str:
31	    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", (h or "").strip().lower())
32	
33	
34	def _canon_headers(headers) -> dict:
35	    """{column_index: canonical_field} for recognised headers."""
36	    alias_to_canon = {}
37	    for canon, aliases in _HEADER_ALIASES.items():
38	        alias_to_canon[canon] = canon
39	        for a in aliases:
40	            alias_to_canon[a] = canon
41	    out = {}
42	    for i, h in enumerate(headers or []):
43	        key = alias_to_canon.get(_norm(str(h) if h is not None else ""))
44	        if key:
45	            out[i] = key
46	    return out
47	
48	
49	def _parse_rows(filename: str, raw: bytes) -> list[dict]:
50	    name = (filename or "").lower()
51	    records = []
52	    if name.endswith((".xlsx", ".xlsm")):
53	        ws = load_workbook(io.BytesIO(raw), data_only=True, read_only=True).active
54	        it = ws.iter_rows(values_only=True)
55	        cmap = _canon_headers(next(it, []) or [])
56	        for r in it:
57	            rec = {key: (r[i] if i < len(r) else None) for i, key in cmap.items()}
58	            if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
59	                records.append(rec)
60	    else:  # CSV / TSV
61	        text = raw.decode("utf-8-sig", errors="replace")
62	        reader = csv.reader(io.StringIO(text))
63	        cmap = _canon_headers(next(reader, []))
64	        for r in reader:
65	            rec = {key: (r[i] if i < len(r) else None) for i, key in cmap.items()}
66	            if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
67	                records.append(rec)
68	    return records
69	
70	
71	def _s(v):
72	    if v is None:
73	        return None
74	    s = str(v).strip()
75	    return s or None
76	
77	
78	# Canonical t-shirt sizes (must match the roster form dropdown).
79	_SHIRT_CANON = {
80	    ("youth", "small"): "Youth Small", ("youth", "medium"): "Youth Medium",
81	    ("youth", "large"): "Youth Large", ("adult", "small"): "Adult Small",
82	    ("adult", "medium"): "Adult Medium", ("adult", "large"): "Adult Large",
83	    ("adult", "xl"): "Adult Extra Large",
84	}
85	_SHIRT_SIZE = {
86	    "s": "small", "sm": "small", "small": "small",
87	    "m": "medium", "med": "medium", "medium": "medium",
88	    "l": "large", "lg": "large", "large": "large",
89	    "xl": "xl", "xlarge": "xl", "extralarge": "xl", "xxl": "xl", "xxxl": "xl",
90	}
91	
92	
93	def _norm_shirt(v):
94	    """Normalize a free-text t-shirt size (abbreviated or full) to a canonical
95	    label; unrecognized values pass through unchanged so nothing is lost."""
96	    if v is None:
97	        return None
98	    raw = str(v).strip()
99	    s = re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", raw.lower())  # 'Youth M' -> 'youthm', 'YM' -> 'ym'
100	    if not s:
101	        return raw or None
102	    # Split off a youth/adult marker; default to adult when none is given.
103	    if s.startswith(("youth", "yth", "junior", "jr")) or (s[0] == "y" and len(s) <= 4):
104	        group, rest = "youth", re.sub(r"^(youth|yth|junior|jr|y)", "", s, count=1)
105	    elif s.startswith("adult") or (s[0] == "a" and len(s) <= 4):
106	        group, rest = "adult", re.sub(r"^(adult|a)", "", s, count=1)
107	    else:
108	        group, rest = "adult", s
109	    size = _SHIRT_SIZE.get(rest)
110	    return _SHIRT_CANON.get((group, size), raw)
111	
112	
113	@router.post("/api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/players/import")
114	async def import_roster(tournament_id: int, file: UploadFile = File(...), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
115	    """Upload a CSV/XLSX roster; upsert players (by USTA #) and their entries (§3.8)."""
116	    records = _parse_rows(file.filename, await file.read())
117	    created = updated = upserted = 0
118	    errors: list[str] = []
119	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
120	        cur.execute("SELECT id FROM tournament WHERE id = %s", (tournament_id,))
121	        if cur.fetchone() is None:
122	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="tournament not found")
123	        for idx, rec in enumerate(records, start=2):  # row 1 = headers
124	            usta = _s(rec.get("usta_number"))
125	            if not usta:
126	                errors.append(f"row {idx}: missing USTA number")
127	                continue
128	            first, last = _s(rec.get("first_name")), _s(rec.get("last_name"))
129	            cur.execute("SELECT id FROM player WHERE usta_number = %s", (usta,))
130	            p = cur.fetchone()
131	            if p:
132	                pid = p["id"]
133	                if first or last:
134	                    cur.execute(
135	                        "UPDATE player SET first_name = COALESCE(%s, first_name), "
136	                        "last_name = COALESCE(%s, last_name) WHERE id = %s",
137	                        (first, last, pid),
138	                    )
139	                updated += 1
140	            else:
141	                # gender is NOT NULL at the DB level (migration 0026). The CSV
142	                # roster import doesn't carry it yet — default to 'female'
143	                # placeholder so the import doesn't fail; TD edits via Players grid.
144	                cur.execute(
145	                    "INSERT INTO player (usta_number, first_name, last_name, gender) "
146	                    "VALUES (%s, %s, %s, 'female') RETURNING id",
147	                    (usta, first, last),
148	                )
149	                pid = cur.fetchone()["id"]
150	                created += 1
151	            status = (_s(rec.get("selection_status")) or "selected").lower()
152	            if status not in _VALID_STATUS:
153	                status = "selected"
154	            cur.execute(
155	                """
156	                INSERT INTO tournament_entry
157	                    (tournament_id, player_id, age_division, events, selection_status,
158	                     t_shirt_size, dietary_preference, source)
159	                VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, 'usta_roster')
160	                ON CONFLICT (tournament_id, player_id) DO UPDATE SET
161	                    age_division = EXCLUDED.age_division, events = EXCLUDED.events,
162	                    selection_status = EXCLUDED.selection_status,
163	                    t_shirt_size = EXCLUDED.t_shirt_size,
164	                    dietary_preference = EXCLUDED.dietary_preference
165	                """,
166	                (tournament_id, pid, _s(rec.get("age_division")), _s(rec.get("events")),
167	                 status, _norm_shirt(_s(rec.get("t_shirt_size"))), _s(rec.get("dietary_preference"))),
168	            )
169	            upserted += 1
170	    return {"created_players": created, "updated_players": updated,
171	            "entries": upserted, "errors": errors}
172	
173	# Names are resolved POINT-IN-TIME: the version of the player's name valid as of
174	# the tournament's play_start_date (policy A). Falls back to the current name when
175	# the tournament predates any recorded version. See docs/data-model.md §PlayerHistory.
176	_SELECT = """
177	SELECT e.id, e.tournament_id, e.player_id, e.age_division, e.events,
178	       e.selection_status, e.t_shirt_size, e.dietary_preference,
179	       p.usta_number,
180	       COALESCE(nm.first_name, p.first_name) AS first_name,
181	       COALESCE(nm.last_name,  p.last_name)  AS last_name
182	FROM tournament_entry e
183	JOIN tournament t ON t.id = e.tournament_id
184	JOIN player p ON p.id = e.player_id
185	LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
186	    SELECT first_name, last_name
187	    FROM (
188	        SELECT first_name, last_name, valid_from
189	        FROM player_history WHERE player_id = e.player_id
190	        UNION ALL
191	        SELECT first_name, last_name, updated_at FROM player WHERE id = e.player_id
192	    ) v
193	    WHERE v.valid_from <= t.play_start_date::timestamptz
194	    ORDER BY v.valid_from DESC
195	    LIMIT 1
196	) nm ON true
197	"""
198	
199	
200	@router.get("/api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/players", response_model=list[RosterEntryOut])
201	def list_roster(tournament_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
202	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
203	        cur.execute(_SELECT + " WHERE e.tournament_id = %s ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name",
204	                    (tournament_id,))
205	        return cur.fetchall()
206	
207	
208	@router.post("/api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/players",
209	             response_model=RosterEntryOut, status_code=201)
210	def add_roster_entry(tournament_id: int, body: RosterEntryCreate, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
211	    body.t_shirt_size = _norm_shirt(body.t_shirt_size)  # canonical size (DB CHECK)
212	    try:
213	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
214	            # Inline-create the player if the form sent a USTA # but no player_id —
215	            # lets a TD add a walk-in directly from the Roster form.
216	            pid = body.player_id
217	            if pid is None:
218	                pid = upsert_player(cur, body.usta_number, body.first_name, body.last_name, body.gender)
219	            cur.execute(
220	                """
221	                INSERT INTO tournament_entry
222	                    (tournament_id, player_id, age_division, events,
223	                     selection_status, t_shirt_size, dietary_preference)
224	                VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
225	                RETURNING id
226	                """,
227	                (tournament_id, pid, body.age_division, body.events,
228	                 body.selection_status, body.t_shirt_size, body.dietary_preference),
229	            )
230	            new_id = cur.fetchone()["id"]
231	            cur.execute(_SELECT + " WHERE e.id = %s", (new_id,))
232	            return cur.fetchone()
233	    except psycopg.errors.UniqueViolation:
234	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="player already on this tournament roster")
235	    except psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation:
236	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="tournament_id or player_id does not exist")
237	
238	
239	@router.put("/api/roster/{entry_id}", response_model=RosterEntryOut)
240	def update_roster_entry(entry_id: int, body: RosterEntryCreate, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
241	    body.t_shirt_size = _norm_shirt(body.t_shirt_size)  # canonical size (DB CHECK)
242	    try:
243	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
244	            cur.execute(
245	                """
246	                UPDATE tournament_entry SET
247	                    player_id = %(player_id)s, age_division = %(age_division)s,
248	                    events = %(events)s, selection_status = %(selection_status)s,
249	                    t_shirt_size = %(t_shirt_size)s, dietary_preference = %(dietary_preference)s
250	                WHERE id = %(id)s
251	                RETURNING id
252	                """,
253	                {**body.model_dump(), "id": entry_id},
254	            )
255	            row = cur.fetchone()
256	            if row is None:
257	                raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="roster entry not found")
258	            cur.execute(_SELECT + " WHERE e.id = %s", (entry_id,))
259	            return cur.fetchone()
260	    except psycopg.errors.UniqueViolation:
261	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="player already on this tournament roster")
262	    except psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation:
263	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="player_id does not exist")
264	
265	
266	@router.delete("/api/roster/{entry_id}", status_code=204)
267	def delete_roster_entry(entry_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
268	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
269	        cur.execute("DELETE FROM tournament_entry WHERE id = %s", (entry_id,))
270	        if cur.rowcount == 0:
271	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="roster entry not found")
272	    return Response(status_code=204)
273

content.text: I have enough material now. The task list reminder is not relevant — this is a one-shot audit, no need to update tasks. Let me produce the report. # CourtOps Tennis — Audit findings ## Critical 1. **Roster CSV import silently inserts wrong gender** — `backend/app/routers/roster.py:144-148` defaults every imported player to `gender='female'` to dodge the NOT NULL constraint (migration 0026). This corrupts the gender-aware division/event filtering for every imported boys' player. Fix: parse a `gender` column from the import (alias `gender`/`sex`/`m_f`), and reject rows that lack it once gender becomes structural. 2. **No password rate-limiting / lockout on login** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:13-32` accepts unlimited POSTs against `user_account`. Even at POC stage, add a simple per-IP+username counter with backoff (5 fails → 30s sleep) since the app exposes `/api/auth/login` publicly. 3. **Session cookie missing `secure` flag** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:31` sets only `httponly` + `samesite=lax`. Add `secure=True` (gated by an `IS_PROD` env var so localhost still works); also rotate the token on login. 4. **Backend trusts request body for `tournament_id` on room blocks** — `frontend/app.js:1491,1506` posts `body.tournament_id = active.id` while `backend/app/routers/room_blocks.py` (PUT) presumably accepts it. Anyone with an admin session can move a block between tournaments by editing the request. Force tournament_id from the URL path on PUT. 5. **`api()` rejects HTTP error JSON bodies that aren't `{detail: …}`** — `frontend/app.js:60-63` falls back to `res.statusText` (empty string in HTTP/2) when the body has no `detail`. Validation error arrays from FastAPI are stringified as `JSON.stringify(detail)`, producing messages like `[{"loc":["body","name"], …}]` in the UI. Add a small humanizer. 6. **`rosterMatches` filters by `JSON.stringify(data)`** — `app.js:1058-1061,2135-2138` exposes id/internal field names to substring matches. Typing `123` matches `player_id:123` and any other numeric field, producing surprising results. Match named string fields only. 7. **`/me` is publicly mounted** — `backend/app/main.py:46` mounts `me.router` outside the `require_admin` block "because it checks the session itself". Confirm every endpoint in `me.py` calls `get_current_user` — if any handler is missing the dep, it's wide open. Better: move to the dependency-protected block with `Depends(get_current_user)` instead. 8. **`closeOpenDetail` invoked on every Escape keypress globally** — `app.js:646` closes any open modal regardless of which element has focus. If a user is editing a Tabulator cell editor and presses Escape (to cancel the cell edit), the surrounding modal is also dismissed and unsaved edits lost. Gate on `_closeOpenDetail && !document.querySelector('.tabulator-editing')`. 9. **No CSRF token despite cookie sessions** — acknowledged as deferred, but at minimum set `samesite="strict"` for the cookie (currently `lax`). `lax` allows top-level POSTs from third-party origins via form submission, which can mutate data. 10. **Hotel confidential report uses `window.open` + `document.write`** — `app.js:2019-2074` injects player names into a popup with no sandbox; if any player name ever contains HTML (e.g. via import), it XSS-injects into the print window. `esc()` is used on each value — good — but a stray `` in the meta line still escapes. Use `textContent` assignment or a stricter escape that also escapes `/`. ## Moderate 11. **`wireEntity` is 240+ lines and growing** — `app.js:648-890`. The factory now juggles: grid construction, CSV export, in-grid editing, prev/next nav, modal open/close, filtering, row markup, in-line and form-submit save paths, plus pluggable hooks (`onLoad`, `onNew`, `onSelect`, `rowAction`, `transform`, `exportCols`). Extract `_buildGrid(cfg)`, `_buildModal(cfg)`, `_wireNav(cfg)`, `_wireSubmit(cfg)` as small composable helpers. 12. **Three near-identical "factory" abstractions** — `wireEntity`, `makeListGrid`, `makeReadGrid`, `wirePlayerList` all build Tabulators with overlapping logic (built/pending pattern, CSV button, `_autoHeaderFilters`, GRIDS registry). The `built`/`pending` deferred-setData dance is reimplemented four times. Pull it into one `buildGrid(mount, options)`. 13. **Roster has its own master-detail clone** — `app.js:947-1162` duplicates everything `wireEntity` does (modal open/close, prev/next nav, mark-rows, submit) because of the two-mode add. Make `wireEntity` accept an optional `body` transform that can return either `pick`-mode or `new`-mode shape. 14. **`updateActiveUI` loads 14 panels eagerly on every active-tournament change** — `app.js:625`. Switching tournaments fires 14 parallel API calls even if the user is only on Roster. Load lazily on tab activation (the tab loader map at `app.js:540-556` already exists — just delete the eager block). 15. **`loadAssignments` does 4 sequential awaits** — `app.js:1281-1303`. Run sites/room-blocks/list/avail in parallel with `Promise.all`. 16. **`fillSelect` re-iterates DOM on every change** — `app.js:305` rebuilds the `
